heading: What is a search score?
body:
Each document in the query results receives a relevancy score that
orders results from highest to lowest relevance. In the simplest
form of scoring, documents score higher if the query term appears
frequently in a document and lower if the query term appears across
many documents in the collection. You can also customize scoring
to tailor search to a specific domain by boosting, decaying, or
modifying the relevance-based default score.

To learn more, see [relevance scoring](scoring-ref).
